heading: Promoting efficient declassification review
label: 3350a Promoting efficient declassification review
notes: References in TextExecutive Order 13526, referred to in subsec. (a), is Ex. Ord. No. 13526, , 75 F.R. 707, 1013, which is set out as a note under .Dec. 29, 2009section 3161 of this title
DefinitionsFor definition of “congressional intelligence committees” as used in this section, see , set out as a note under .section 7002 of Pub. L. 118–31section 3003 of this title

children.text: makes a certification to Congress, including the congressional intelligence committees, the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs, the Committee on Foreign Relations, the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate, and the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Oversight and Accountability, the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives, that the declassification of certain components within the record would be harmful to the protection of sources and methods or national security, pursuant to existing processes; and

provides an explanation to Congress, including the congressional intelligence committees, the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs, the Committee on Foreign Relations, the Committee on the Judiciary of the Senate, and the Committee on Armed Services, the Committee on Oversight and Accountability, the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and the Committee on the Judiciary of the House of Representatives, for such certification.

children.heading: Application
children.label: b Application
children.text: Subsection (a) shall apply regardless of whether or not the record described in such subsection is in the legal custody of the National Archives and Records Administration.
